instructions.name: Spec Review — Technical Specification Gate
instructions.objective: Produce a technical specification that bridges the story (WHAT) to implementation (HOW). The spec must be approved before dev-story can begin.

llm.mandate: Stories define WHAT to build. Specs define HOW to build it. Both are required.
No implementation without an approved spec. This gate is non-negotiable.

row.excuse: This story is too simple for a spec
row.reality: Simple stories with specs take 10 minutes. Simple stories without specs cause rework.

row.excuse: I already know how to build this
row.reality: Knowledge is not documentation. Write it down so others can review it.

row.excuse: The story file IS the spec
row.reality: Stories define WHAT and acceptance criteria. Specs define HOW — components, data model, slices.
Rework from missing specs slows you down 3-5x more. Measure twice, cut once.
Post-hoc docs describe what was built, not what should be built. The spec prevents wrong turns.
Review without a written spec is verbal agreement. Write it down.

step.goal: Analyze the story and surface all technical decisions
step.action: Load the story file from {story_file}. Read it completely.
Load {project_context} if it exists — this is the authoritative reference for patterns and structure.
Invoke protocol: discover_inputs (load architecture and PRD docs if available)
For each acceptance criterion in the story, identify:
- Which components/files will be affected
- What data model changes are needed (migrations, RLS policies, indexes)
- What API changes are needed (new endpoints, modified contracts, schema changes)
- What testing approach is required
Surface hidden assumptions — things the story takes for granted that could go wrong:
- Dependencies on other stories or features not yet built
- Assumptions about existing API behavior or data shapes
- Performance implications at scale
- Edge cases not covered by acceptance criteria
step.template-output: Generate the Objective section of the spec
Generate Components Affected with specific file paths
Generate Data Model Changes (or mark N/A)

step.goal: Break the spec into thin vertical implementation slices
step.action: Slice the work VERTICALLY, not horizontally. Each slice should deliver a thin end-to-end piece of functionality.
Target ~100 lines per slice. If a slice exceeds 200 lines, split it.
For each slice, define:
- What it delivers (user-visible behavior or internal contract)
- Files to create or modify
- Dependencies on other slices
- Estimated size: XS (1 file), S (2-3 files), M (4-5 files), L (6-7 files), XL (8+ files — must be split)
Order slices by dependency graph — foundational slices first, UI/integration slices last.
Identify which slices can be parallelized.

step.goal: Validate spec against project standards and security requirements
step.action: Cross-check against {project_context} patterns. Flag any deviations with justification.
Verify every acceptance criterion from the story is covered by at least one implementation slice.
Load {security_gate} and run the universal security checklist against proposed changes:
- Input validation: Are all new inputs validated?
- Authorization: Are data access patterns properly scoped?
- Data exposure: Could any new API response leak sensitive data?
- Secrets: Are any new secrets/credentials needed? How managed?
- Injection: Are all new queries parameterized?
Define boundaries:
- ALWAYS DO: Things that must happen regardless of implementation choices
- ASK FIRST: Decisions that need stakeholder input before proceeding
- NEVER DO: Anti-patterns and out-of-scope changes to explicitly avoid
step.template-output: Generate Boundaries (Always Do / Ask First / Never Do)

step.action: Present the complete spec document for review.
The spec MUST be explicitly approved before dev-story workflow can begin.
step.ask: Spec is complete. Review and choose: [A] Approve — spec is ready for implementation [R] Revise — send back with specific feedback [E] Escalate — needs CEO or architect review

check.action: Mark spec as APPROVED with date and reviewer
Save final spec to output file
Report: Spec approved. Ready for /team:dev (dev-story workflow).
Collect feedback and revise the relevant sections
Write escalation request to team/_memory/_comms/requests/ for Tanto
Report: Spec escalated for review. Awaiting approval before implementation can begin.
